name: The White House
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f3e1b42b27b92b6e634e02ed29e94610d4e317dd0c0b568530f87f8b382a5a7a970b0d472f13925c9b18eb19a2d659d3cad679fa8d49c27d
Trump says he'll visit Florida, emphasize mental health after school shooting

- POLITICO

On Thursday, President Donald J. Trump addressed the Nation and announced his plans to visit Florida after a gunman opened fire at a high school in Broward County, resulting in 17 deaths. President Trump’s remarks underscored his commitment to “working with state and local leaders to help secure our schools and tackle the difficult issue of mental health.” The President also ordered U.S. flags to be flown at half-staff through sunset on Monday.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f3e1b42b27b92b6e5c93fdf90a5977a852a5c83935944ffb3d96a8a8638eee1f013e828f36b52d34a6b22cc7d0a36a13542d8abf27080667

U.S. homebuilder sentiment remains near highest level since 1999

- Bloomberg Markets

According to the latest data from the National Association of Home Builders and Wells Fargo, “confidence among American homebuilders held steady in February near the highest level since 1999.” This new report signals that “home construction will continue to pick up as economic conditions remain favorable.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f3e1b42b27b92b6e4826748e7c7ec2ce941db2fb4aa2a9d8d6f1182fbda61be9e34bd31583b9ddd0e9bcd5c15a9a24b68fdc8a29231c6e15
Building a stronger America

- WhiteHouse.gov

“A Nation’s infrastructure is a measure of its greatness,” National Economic Council Director Gary Cohn writes. He breaks down the infrastructure plan that President Trump unveiled this week—a main goal of which is to stimulate state, local, and private investment. “Rather than telling governors and mayors what to do, we will partner with them as they invest in the most pressing projects,” Cohn says.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f3e1b42b27b92b6eff6232d7c80a6115c2d437a4d0794d68c02d8ca0d927fc01f4fdc06d4185a2dd8848f8d52653ffd28852327f56becae0

Democrats scramble on taxes as Republicans gain steam

CNN

The Democrats have been caught “flat-footed by the suddenly increasing popularity of the GOP tax plan,” CNN reports. Democratic leadership has been in a “scramble” trying to “reignite opposition to a tax plan . . . [that] is now seen favorably by about half of voters.” Democrats did not anticipate that “businesses would quickly hand out sizable bonus checks in response to its implementation.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f3e1b42b27b92b6e12fc72c7e6e56e6ade882ca0cfec1b75df693ec560a6bd9258a0d3e0414b890777a39857cec76af1a82ad7bec06765b2
Sen. Tim Scott: New tax law will spur opportunity in distressed communities

- USA Today

Sen. Tim Scott (R-SC) met with President Trump, Ivanka Trump, and others in the Oval Office this week to discuss how “Opportunity Zones” could be a game-changer in confronting poverty in America. “The Investing in Opportunity Act gives investors incentive to put their sidelined capital gains into struggling areas in every zip code,” Sen. Scott writes.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f3e1b42b27b92b6e9dbeaee73989be204255681ed63be544f499105f68b43a3e14f673fbc9e213ff128879794047d52f5b3229652d48bb96
FERC moves to help solar, wind power by removing barrier to energy storage

- Washington Examiner

In the Trump Administration’s latest regulatory rollback, the Federal Energy Regulatory Committee removed “long-standing barriers to energy storage in power markets,” a move that could help the country better utilize its solar and wind energy resources.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f3e1b42b27b92b6e69e3344873059fe3f7172066b1d9b26d3b6049ffffb6f2cda5ef4bd603951f6f06fceefce031cd055e980cc58341d58e
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f3e1b42b27b92b6e32423a4b58b150401e6f16b407b335153f6331bcebdca537c7a5d54a751f7bea5e7448280ecb91088768fc00e76ce08c
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d42624db9948540e039e4369a74d6968d61d7c1fce08243718291aa8fcfc6060c20a4d4a805c96bc13c59d71f3bfa0955dcd535e9ef5efbdd3
Unsubscribe

name: Donna Edwards
subject: Fwd: We deserve to have a voice
body:
[](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff7c/767134fb/5cc9baad/43382d78/2037107172/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=rtw_180217_schoolboardnatlkicker_em&recurring=true")
Friends,
Did you see my message from Wednesday?
Our community should be able to hold our school board accountable for the decisions they make regarding our children's futures.
We should be investing in our students and giving them the tools they need to succeed, not closing more schools and packing classrooms.
**[Make a contribution now to join me in fighting for an accountable, elected school board for Prince George's County.](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff7c/767134fb/5cc9baad/43382d79/2037107172/VEsE/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=rtw_180217_schoolboardnatlkicker_em&recurring=true")**
Thanks,
Donna
\---Forwarded Message---
Friends,
In most places, when you send your child to public school, you're putting your child's future in the hands of someone you've had a say in hiring.
But in Prince George's County, parents and teachers have no say in who's making the decisions that affect our children -- we have a school board that is appointed, rather than elected.
**[Shouldn't parents have a voice in this process? Make a $25 contribution now to help me advocate for an elected school board that parents and teachers can hold accountable.](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff7c/767134fb/5cc9baad/43382d78/2037107172/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=rtw_180217_schoolboardnatlkicker_em&recurring=true")**
As Prince George's County Executive, I will advocate for a transition to an elected school board so that parents and teachers have a say in the decisions affecting our children.
We should be investing in our children's education and providing our teachers with enough resources to give each child the individual attention they need to learn, grow, and thrive.
But the unelected and unaccountable school board wants to close schools and pack more students into classrooms that are already bursting at the seams. From grade changes to graduation rates to how your taxes are spent, you deserve answers.
Without parents and teachers being a part of the conversation, the unelected school board can make decisions about our children's futures without any accountability.
**[Will you make a $25 contribution to my campaign now so I can continue to advocate for an accountable, elected school board in Prince George's County?](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff7c/767134fb/5cc9baad/43382d78/2037107172/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=rtw_180217_schoolboardnatlkicker_em&recurring=true")**
We have to protect our children and our future,
Donna
**[dONATE](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff7c/767134fb/5cc9baad/43382d78/2037107172/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=rtw_180217_schoolboardnatlkicker_em&recurring=true")**

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
Email is the best way to stay in contact with supporters like you, but if you wish to unsubscribe from all emails, please [click here.](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff7c/767134fb/5cc9baad/43382d76/2037107172/VEsA/ "http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/unsubscribe")

Authority: Donna for Prince George's, Glenn Ivey, Treasurer
P.O. Box 44343
Fort Washington, MD 20749

name: Ben Jealous
subject: "Agitate! Agitate! Agitate!"
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
We must find the courage to do what we know is right for ourselves, our neighbors, and our children's future - and it's why I'm running to be your next governor of Maryland
#
Friend -
This week marks what would have been Frederick Douglass' 200th birthday.
**To commemorate this, and Black History Month, I wrote a piece for The Root to discuss the work left to be done in our progressive movements, and the dire need to continue to "agitate". Read the article below, then chip in what you can to help us continue the work:**
Onward,
Ben

**[DONATE](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a71d/7ab0e2c4/7f4be659/349a1d1f/3554350597/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst-bhm18?refcode=OHQ02148B")**
* * *
**Ben Jealous Reminds Us to 'Agitate!' on Frederick Douglass' 200th Birthday**
A few weeks before his death, 77-year-old Frederick Douglass was asked what advice he would give to a young black American.
"Agitate! Agitate! Agitate!" was his reply.
This pioneer of civil rights lived long enough to die of a heart attack in his old age, a heroic feat in and of itself for a former slave. Douglass was born on Maryland's Eastern Shore 200 years ago today, Feb. 14, and it is my belief that he went on to become the greatest Marylander to have ever lived. His accomplishments would stand out in any era, but when put in the context of Douglass' having been a former slave, he was the epitome of what we call "black excellence" today.
**[DONATE](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a71d/7ab0e2c4/7f4be659/349a1d1f/3554350597/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst-bhm18?refcode=OHQ02148B")**
Douglass was a banker, a lawyer, an abolitionist, an escaped slave, the most-photographed American of the 19th century, and a leader who would help hold America together as a civil war threatened to tear it apart.
He had the courage to stand up for women's rights, Native American rights, and immigrant rights even as his own rights were still being denied.
When Douglass attended the Seneca Falls Convention in 1848, he was the only African American to do so. As collected members voiced opposition to female suffrage, he admonished them, saying: "In the denial of the right to participate in government, not merely the degradation of woman and the perpetuation of a great injustice happens, but the maiming and repudiation of one-half of the moral and intellectual power of the government of the world."
He said he would find himself unable to accept the right to vote as a black man if women could not also claim that right, tying the struggle for his right to vote to that of female suffrage, a unifying vision of universal suffrage rather than one that pitted two marginalized groups against one another.
**[DONATE](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a71d/7ab0e2c4/7f4be659/349a1d1f/3554350597/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst-bhm18?refcode=OHQ02148B")**
Douglass also spoke out against the Chinese Exclusion Act, which was the first law to explicitly prevent a specific ethnic group from immigrating to the United States.
In opposition Douglass said:
> _"Our geographical position, our relation to the outside world, our fundamental principles of government, world-embracing in their scope and character, our vast resources, requiring all manner of labor to develop them, and our already existing composite population, all conspire to one grand end, and that is, to make us the perfect national illustration of the unity and dignity of the human family that the world has ever seen."_
As we celebrate Douglass' 200th birthday, his legacy endures in the faces of all those who continue to agitate, agitate, agitate in pursuit of a more just and fair America. Douglass' influence was in the actions of lawyers who flocked to American airports to help immigrants caught in Donald Trump's Muslim ban. We saw it in the marchers who descended upon Charlottesville , Va., to say no to the Ku Klux Klan, and in the voices of all those who participated in last year's Women's March to agitate against the election of a man who bragged about sexually assaulting women.
Throughout my career as a civil rights leader and community organizer, I've often thought of Douglass and tried to mirror his example. Essentially, at his core, Douglass was an organizer and a believer in building big broad coalitions for change. I was fortunate enough to be named Marylander of the Year in 2012, because as president and CEO of the NAACP, I helped lead a historic effort to abolish the death penalty, achieve marriage equality and pass the Maryland DREAM Act all in one year. When skeptics doubted if immigrants, LGBT and civil rights groups could all come together and advance at once, I thought of Douglass' courage and worked to help fashion the type of coalition he described as "the perfect national illustration of the unity and dignity of the human family the world has ever seen."
His refusal to see opportunity and justice as a zero-sum game gives us a road map for every struggle for equality since. When we act as if only one group has the ability to gain power or that the success of other oppressed groups comes at the price of another, we allow ourselves to be pitted against those who would ally with us to ensure justice for all.
This Valentine's Day and in honor of his birthday, let's remember Douglass' advice to agitate in the face of injustice and his courage in saying we are stronger when we act as one.
**[DONATE](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a71d/7ab0e2c4/7f4be659/349a1d1f/3554350597/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst-bhm18?refcode=OHQ02148B")**

* * *

name: gina.raimondo
subject: I just wanted to tell you about my week
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Gina Raimondo
body:
Friend,
The opioid epidemic is our nation's most serious public health crisis. In Rhode Island, we are doing everything in our power to address the challenge and save lives. We're the only state that provides addiction support and treatment for people while they are in prison and after they are released. Earlier this week, a national medical journal reported that Rhode Island has successfully decreased overdose deaths for people leaving prison by 61 percent.
Addiction is tragic -- especially when babies and children are involved. This week, I was excited to announce several new approaches to supporting pregnant and parenting families in treatment and recovery.
We're stepping up efforts to ensure that first responders in communities across our state have access to the life-saving drug Naloxone. We've also created a hotline -- 942-STOP -- to connect people in need with available resources.
That's progress. But, there's more work to do. We won't rest in our efforts to address opioid addiction and overdose.
Gina

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: He can't have it both ways
body:
Here at your Indiana Republican Party, we received a strange press release from Democrat Joe Donnelly this week (we read them so you don't have to... | | |
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0260d4ed32&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_He Can't Have it Both Ways_**
Here at _**your**_ Indiana Republican Party, we received a strange press release from Democrat Joe Donnelly this week (we read them so you don't have to). In the press release, Donnelly -- who is leading the Democrat ticket this year -- decided to tout a new study that would seemingly show that he votes in line with Republican**President Donald Trump's** priorities 62 percent of the time.

There's only one problem: Donnelly's supposed "support" for the president doesn't have a connection to reality. Depending on where he is and who he's with, you'll hear from a different Democrat Donnelly. And, let's be clear, the Donnelly who is is voting in DC isn't standing by our president when it matters most.

Here in Indiana, you'll read news stories about the "moderate" Donnelly who says things like, "before they were ever Trump voters, they were my voters" and sends out misnomer press releases, like the one we got this week. But then when he thinks Hoosiers aren't listening, he goes on the radio with folks like former Obama aide David Axelrod and says that Trump voters aren't "who America is." He travels across the country raising money alongside his Washington boss Chuck Schumer, and takes orders from him directly when he's told he needs to vote against cutting federal taxes for Hoosiers.

And what about that 62 percent figure? Well, when you cut out votes on confirming cabinet members and high-level federal positions (which historically receive bipartisan support), that "votes with Trump" figure goes down to a dismal 35 percent. And that's just not acceptable.

We'll continue to keep you updated through November on these repeated times when Democrat Donnelly says one thing in Indiana, and does something different out in Washington. With great Republicans like you on the watch, Donnelly's going to learn in November that he can't have it both ways.

-Kyle Hupfer

_Picture: Last weekend, Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer joined Congressman Larry Bucshon and Gibson County GOP Chairman CT Montgomery for their annual Lincoln Day Dinner._
---
| |
---
| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8cb3d9cd30&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Building Indiana's 21st Century Workforce_** Here in Indiana, we're lucky to have a booming economy that continues to outpace the nation. And while companies are committing to create record numbers of new Hoosier jobs, Governor Holcomb is also making a big commitment. He's working with Hoosier employers and local leaders from across the state to make sure we have the systems in place to help Hoosiers fill the high-demand, high-wage jobs not just of today, but of the future.

And this was a big week for putting the spotlight on Indiana's workforce development priorities. This week, Governor Holcomb:
* Met with the Terre Haute Chamber of Commerce at the Statehouse, and spoke at the Indiana Chamber Legislative Dinner
* Discussed workplace initiatives with the team at Honda's assembly facility in Greensburg
* Toured the Vincennes University Hass Technical Training and Education Center in Lebanon to hear from students who are receiving free training and certifications through the Next Level Jobs initiative
* Visited the Columbus Learning Center to hear from students, leaders and partners in their work to advance opportunities for Hoosiers throughout the region.

If you or someone you know is interested in receiving free workforce training in high-demand industries like advanced manufacturing, life sciences, tech, or logistics, visit [NextLevelJobs.org](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1219031ea9&e=1acd9bb3d6) to learn more!

_Picture: Thursday night, Governor Holcomb headlined the Boone County Lincoln Day Dinner, alongside Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer. _
---
|
---
|
---
| **_Thank you, Senator Long_** This week, President Pro Tem David Long announced his plans to retire from the Indiana Senate later this year. With a career of more than 20 years in public service at the Statehouse and more than a decade as the Senate's leader, David Long has played a critical role in transforming Indiana from a middle-of-the-pack state into a national example of great government.

Thank you, Senator Long, for your service to Indiana! Your knowledge and tireless work on behalf of Hoosiers will be missed!
---
|
---

clean_body: Here at your Indiana Republican Party, we received a strange press release from Democrat Joe Donnelly this week (we read them so you don't have to... | | | | | | | He Can't Have it Both Ways Here at your Indiana Republican Party, we received a strange press release from Democrat Joe Donnelly this week (we read them so you don't have to). In the press release, Donnelly -- who is leading the Democrat ticket this year -- decided to tout a new study that would seemingly show that he votes in line with RepublicanPresident Donald Trump's priorities 62 percent of the time. There's only one problem: Donnelly's supposed "support" for the president doesn't have a connection to reality. Depending on where he is and who he's with, you'll hear from a different Democrat Donnelly. And, let's be clear, the Donnelly who is is voting in DC isn't standing by our president when it matters most. Here in Indiana, you'll read news stories about the "moderate" Donnelly who says things like, "before they were ever Trump voters, they were my voters" and sends out misnomer press releases, like the one we got this week. But then when he thinks Hoosiers aren't listening, he goes on the radio with folks like former Obama aide David Axelrod and says that Trump voters aren't "who America is." He travels across the country raising money alongside his Washington boss Chuck Schumer, and takes orders from him directly when he's told he needs to vote against cutting federal taxes for Hoosiers. And what about that 62 percent figure? Well, when you cut out votes on confirming cabinet members and high-level federal positions (which historically receive bipartisan support), that "votes with Trump" figure goes down to a dismal 35 percent. And that's just not acceptable. We'll continue to keep you updated through November on these repeated times when Democrat Donnelly says one thing in Indiana, and does something different out in Washington. With great Republicans like you on the watch, Donnelly's going to learn in November that he can't have it both ways. -Kyle Hupfer Picture: Last weekend, Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer joined Congressman Larry Bucshon and Gibson County GOP Chairman CT Montgomery for their annual Lincoln Day Dinner. | | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Building Indiana's 21st Century Workforce Here in Indiana, we're lucky to have a booming economy that continues to outpace the nation. And while companies are committing to create record numbers of new Hoosier jobs, Governor Holcomb is also making a big commitment. He's working with Hoosier employers and local leaders from across the state to make sure we have the systems in place to help Hoosiers fill the high-demand, high-wage jobs not just of today, but of the future. And this was a big week for putting the spotlight on Indiana's workforce development priorities. This week, Governor Holcomb: Met with the Terre Haute Chamber of Commerce at the Statehouse, and spoke at the Indiana Chamber Legislative Dinner Discussed workplace initiatives with the team at Honda's assembly facility in Greensburg Toured the Vincennes University Hass Technical Training and Education Center in Lebanon to hear from students who are receiving free training and certifications through the Next Level Jobs initiative Visited the Columbus Learning Center to hear from students, leaders and partners in their work to advance opportunities for Hoosiers throughout the region. If you or someone you know is interested in receiving free workforce training in high-demand industries like advanced manufacturing, life sciences, tech, or logistics, visit to learn more! Picture: Thursday night, Governor Holcomb headlined the Boone County Lincoln Day Dinner, alongside Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer. | | | Thank you, Senator Long This week, President Pro Tem David Long announced his plans to retire from the Indiana Senate later this year. With a career of more than 20 years in public service at the Statehouse and more than a decade as the Senate's leader, David Long has played a critical role in transforming Indiana from a middle-of-the-pack state into a national example of great government. name: LegitAction Team
subject: Russ Feingold on the Parkland shooting (video)
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

The recent shooting in Parkland, Florida is an example of the deadly
consequences that can arise when the American people overwhelmingly
ask for a policy (like gun safety reforms) that Congress ignores at
the behest of a few campaign donors (like the NRA and gun
manufacturers).

Russ recently went on MSNBC to make it clear: wealthy gun interests
are undermining our democratic legitimacy, and it’s having real-world
consequences.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/5519?t=1002&akid=627%2E356062%2EnLG1jr ]Check out this clip of Russ on MSNBC condemning the lethal
consequences of dark money -- then share it with your family and
friends:

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/5519?t=1003&akid=627%2E356062%2EnLG1jr ]Watch Russ Feingold on MSNBC

Ever since the Citizens United decision unleashed a flood of secret
money into American politics, the NRA and gun manufacturers have
contributed hundreds of millions of dollars to Republican members of
Congress to buy their complicity in thwarting sensible gun reforms.

Something is wrong with our democratic legitimacy when our leaders put
special interests like the gun lobby above protecting American lives.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/5519?t=1004&akid=627%2E356062%2EnLG1jr ]Watch the video today, then share it with your family and friends.

If we want to make progress in this country, we need a government that
is responsive to the will of the people.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today,

LegitAction Team

name: Bernie
subject: Ben & Chuy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
One of the great lessons of our presidential campaign was that we showed candidates don't have to spend all of their time begging millionaires and billionaires for money in order to win
#
Sisters and Brothers -
One of the great lessons of our presidential campaign was that we showed -- in a way that can change politics in America forever -- that candidates don't have to spend all of their time begging millionaires and billionaires for money in order to win.
We proved that if you run a campaign that authentically and unapologetically speaks to the needs of the working people of this country, they will respond overwhelmingly.
My friend **Ben Jealous** is running that kind of campaign as he seeks the Democratic nomination to serve as the next Governor of Maryland. His campaign, like ours, is powered by lots of people giving small amounts of money. He's received more individual donations than any other candidate in the race -- a fact noticed by the special interests likely to spend heavily to stop him. Sound familiar?
**Chuy Garcia** did the same thing when he ran to unseat Rahm Emanuel as Mayor of Chicago. Thanks to the support of working people of all backgrounds across the city, he did better than anyone could have possibly imagined. Today, he's running for Congress in Chicago. His primary is next month, and he needs our help.
**Ben Jealous and Chuy Garcia supported our campaign for president when we needed it most. And right now -- with their Democratic primaries coming up soon -- they both need us. Please help me elect two more allies of our political revolution:**
**[Can you join me in splitting a $3 donation between Chuy Garcia's campaign for Congress in Chicago and Ben Jealous' campaign to serve as the next Governor of Maryland? Both of these elections are coming up pretty soon, so this is important.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-ben-chuy?refcode=em180217-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-ben-chuy?refcode=em180217-full")**
Through their campaigns Chuy and Ben are bringing attention to the grotesque levels of income and wealth inequality in this country. Both are talking about health care as a right for every man, woman and child in America. They are talking about our broken criminal justice system and our people's lack of access to affordable higher education. They are addressing the global crisis of climate change and the need for real comprehensive immigration reform.
Thank you for supporting them with your donation before their primaries.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
****[DONATE](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-ben-chuy?refcode=em180217-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-ben-chuy?refcode=em180217-full")****

Chuy Garcia did the same thing when he ran to unseat Rahm Emanuel as Mayor of Chicago. Thanks to the support of working people of all backgrounds across the city, he did better than anyone could have possibly imagined. Today, he's running for Congress in Chicago. His primary is next month, and he needs our help. Ben Jealous and Chuy Garcia supported our campaign for president when we needed it most. And right now -- with their Democratic primaries coming up soon -- they both need us. Please help me elect two more allies of our political revolution: "" Through their campaigns Chuy and Ben are bringing attention to the grotesque levels of income and wealth inequality in this country. Both are talking about health care as a right for every man, woman and child in America. They are talking about our broken criminal justice system and our people's lack of access to affordable higher education. They are addressing the global crisis of climate change and the need for real comprehensive immigration reform. Thank you for supporting them with your donation before their primaries. In solidarity, Bernie Sanders ** "" ** Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402

Hello,

Our nation and our state are facing a moral crisis with gun violence. This epidemic has taken lives from us at Pulse, Charleston, the Fort Lauderdale Airport, Newtown, Parkland, and countless other places that don't make national news. We grieve with them -- but we know thoughts and prayers are not enough.

We need action, not words.

Following the tragedy on Wednesday, Andrew has been laying out his plans for gun safety proposals -- most recently on MSNBC's _The Last Word with Lawrence O'Donnell_ Thursday night and MSNBC's _AM Joy_ with Joy Reid this morning.

**[Hear Andrew lay out his plans for gun safety proposals, then if you're with us, become a citizen co-sponsor of Andrew's plan for common-sense gun safety now.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31bb/9e00690/4275497c/2403bc97/1227405659/VEsE/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-gun-feb18?utm_campaign=em18_180217_AMJgun_acq_gq_act__RJG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180217_AMJgun_acq_gq_act_BMP&utm_content=2+-+Hear+Andrew+lay+out+his+plans+for+gun+sa")**

For years, Andrew has been standing against the gun lobby and the Republicans who continuously put their needs before saving people's lives -- and now is no different.

Thank you,

Brendan McPhillips

body:
I'll be the first to admit that we send a lot of emails. But it's for a good reason. |

| | Peter,

I'll be the first to admit that we send a lot of emails. But it's for a good reason.

Just this week, the _Indy Star_ reported that the Koch brothers are launching $2 million worth of ads distorting my record of standing up for Hoosier families. If I didn't have such a strong community behind me, I'd have to spend all my time in Washington and on the phone raising enough money to give us a chance of fighting. **But thanks to supporters like you, my team has always been able to count on folks to meet the fundraising goals we need to keep me on the road talking to Hoosier voters.**

This is one of those times when I need your help. We need another 42 supporters to hit our mid-month goal and take the fight to the Kochs, Mitch McConnell, and their special interest friends. **Will you chip in before the midnight deadline?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

| Good Saturday evening!
---
| |
---
First things first:

**Mark your calendars for this Tuesday, and get out and vote for Judge Michael Screnock for Wisconsin Supreme Court in the primary.**

He's the only candidate on the ballot committed to upholding the rule of law, and he needs your support!
---
| |
---
This week, we started off with the news that [First Lady Tonette Walker is supporting Leah's campaign](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=f5d2c535d9e04820abee6d6d225c85d9&_e=X6LNN6K_3uNGVsA5abriR43_KSxjGDqjT-JdYabE9wIkCg07HAgaisq22fqsBEloS-QosEJmeb5w03FbuJDE3ekDpRvkbjzdMm75BnPGHn2B-2QwsvHR5VOfCsBwV5JGTgErYugfaKai4Ev_6cjbyy6h7xB0O1qRLJe76mpE5vNU8VpxV82vCcialpXKO2RRxcI1Nf6qjhz3gkCpvS50udCDUtbcV3zQqmKYAO4wM4wUkXZIJzxzKVhfSvUCqcYJtC9O7oap5s73KgrqXTaQ1g9mwxl8GEraLy2Uoz8OAeaaz_DB9-s7b-FGrgQo5p6JgTpBrGdcdhjKnjfxqUlddk1oLIm2Yevk2xDiY4wwKLG5UN7bwu0SWv7v3A-72ntd), and she'll be hosting a Women for Leah event toward the end of March.

Leah's team is full of Wisconsin-based supporters, like Mrs. Walker, and our focus will continue to be on building a homegrown grassroots campaign.
---
| On Valentine’s Day, Leah sent a gift of custom candy hearts to Tammy Baldwin. The custom hearts were marked with items liberal politicians such as Baldwin adore — taxes, government shutdowns and the swamp.

The card read: “Tammy, These break my heart, but I know you love them! Happy Valentine’s Day! – Leah.”
---
|
---
---
| Some of Leah's supporters on campus from UW–Madison helped deliver the Valentine to Sen. Baldwin's state office.
---
|
---
---
| At the state Capitol in Madison, Leah was able to testify on her bill that would repeal the minimum markup on prescription drugs and the ban on below-cost sales in Wisconsin. She's authored versions of this bill for several sessions, and it finally got a public hearing!
---
|
---
Photo Credit: MacIver Institute
| "It’s absurd that on Black Friday, or when parents are shopping for school supplies, or senior citizens are purchasing prescription drugs, they will pay more at the checkout," Leah said.
---
| This last week, Leah was in Price, Monroe, Ozaukee, Jefferson, Dane Counties. Tonight she'll be at the Marathon County Lincoln Day Dinner.
---
|
---

## [Wisconsin State Journal: Republican US Senate hopeful Kevin Nicholson still faces questions about his Democratic past](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=f5d2c535d9e04820abee6d6d225c85d9&_e=Wtqdwe8gxWo4V6z6G42CEqS4-qrOwi4xk4X9h5KMx3VF2iqOaXjbIuhAy9_jCyYjLIRLH_FFjjYAIEaGRJ3140snuT4fpU0KWWiPmB-oETy_ZZe9PA3TOY7Qrry0iUKKqEAR4gArA-AMATWzRy_KWiuxZwzN2nECZWMtbEIjH1L2tgd_66rQjLeMECZk3iLj3g4IwE15A7Jmds89tfzhfm_DuazMgfAQNDgW8r15G_qrULni2DvPkgmYobvujum0St4Jn3D86ho8_r4KF6pgaFdnt8pV8L8HdZdXs-B70Qyv6e1YYOqXXnkk68Ytxxhbe051-13lAYzWQ4J9mT0QhOSEI_R9A5CpFa_JMtOpVsx9O8WTuTJcFAzEJGSuR6EZmhjqSHVjlKgZzZy5Pa29aPrEd2kiPYI0mJmgUnBCdTE713Fo9GO01BQTY6FrymDCOJZWDZjCr602wNOXi59XIg%3D%3D)
Republican U.S. Senate candidate Kevin Nicholson has found his Democratic past is not dead — in fact, it’s not even past.

Nicholson, a Delafield management consultant seeking the GOP nod to oppose Democratic Sen. Tammy Baldwin, is still grappling with questions — many from fellow Republicans — about his background, which included a stint as the national College Democrats president.
